critical_patterns: Dual manager pattern (soft-deleted vs all records)
Deferred ForeignKey using string reference to avoid circular imports

file_insights.impact: Activates the core Django app containing Organization model

patterns_discovered.description: Model uses two managers: default soft-delete filtering manager and all_objects for accessing all records including deleted
patterns_discovered.benefit: Ensures accidental queries don't expose deleted data while allowing explicit access when needed

patterns_discovered.description: Functionality separated into abstract mixins (SoftDeleteMixin, TimeStampedMixin) to be composed into models
patterns_discovered.benefit: DRY principle - reusable across multiple models, clean separation of concerns

patterns_discovered.description: Using UUIDField with default=uuid.uuid4 as primary key instead of auto-incrementing integer
patterns_discovered.benefit: Globally unique, scalable across distributed systems, non-guessable IDs

patterns_discovered.description: Flexible JSON storage for organization-specific configuration without schema migrations
patterns_discovered.benefit: Extensible configuration without database schema changes, with getter/setter helper methods

patterns_discovered.description: Using string reference ('subscriptions.Plan') instead of direct import to avoid circular dependency
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables forward references to models in other apps not yet created

patterns_discovered.description: Module-level docstring listing all planned models and critical rules, detailed class docstrings with usage examples
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear intent, constraints, and implementation patterns documented upfront

patterns_discovered.description: Explicit indexes on commonly filtered fields (status + deleted_at compound index, slug index)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Optimizes queries filtering by status/deletion or slug lookups

gotchas_discovered.description: Default queries exclude soft-deleted records, but developers must remember to use all_objects when intentionally querying deleted records
gotchas_discovered.risk: Silent data loss appearance if not understood; deleted records won't show in standard queries
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Comprehensive documentation in module docstring, manager naming convention (all_objects), property-based deletion check

gotchas_discovered.description: Module documentation emphasizes 'EVERY query MUST include organization_id' but this is enforced by developer discipline, not database constraints
gotchas_discovered.risk: Data leakage between tenants if developer forgets organization_id filter
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Clear documentation, future consideration for queryset filtering middleware or custom manager methods

gotchas_discovered.description: Organization.plan references 'subscriptions.Plan' which doesn't exist yet
gotchas_discovered.risk: Migrations will fail if migrations are created before subscriptions app exists; future migration dependencies
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Using string reference prevents import error, but requires careful migration sequencing

gotchas_discovered.description: settings uses default=dict which could theoretically share mutable state
gotchas_discovered.risk: Minor - Django handles this correctly, but non-obvious
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Using default=dict is Django's recommended pattern for JSONField, documented in help_text

gotchas_discovered.issue: No Database-Level Constraint on Tenant Isolation
gotchas_discovered.description: Multi-tenancy architecture relies entirely on application-level filtering
gotchas_discovered.risk: If ORM filters are bypassed (raw SQL), tenant isolation fails
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Documented critical rule, future could add row-level security or middleware enforcement

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created foundational Organization model serving as multi-tenant root entity with comprehensive features and documentation
key_achievements: Implemented UUID primary key for global uniqueness and scalability
Added soft delete functionality via reusable mixins with dual manager pattern
Created timestamp tracking (created_at, updated_at) via mixin
JSONField for flexible organization settings with getter/setter methods
Status lifecycle management (ACTIVE, SUSPENDED, DELETED states)
Comprehensive module and class documentation with usage examples
Database indexes for optimized common queries
Future-proofed with string reference to subscriptions.Plan

recommendations.recommendation: Implement middleware or custom manager to enforce tenant filtering
recommendations.rationale: Currently relies on developer discipline to add organization_id filter to every query; automatic enforcement would prevent data leakage
recommendations.implementation_hint: Create custom QuerySet/Manager that overrides filter() to require organization_id or use middleware to attach organization context

recommendations.recommendation: Create comprehensive test suite for Organization model covering CRUD, soft delete, status transitions, and settings operations
recommendations.rationale: Model has multiple interacting features (soft delete, timestamps, status lifecycle, JSON settings); needs test coverage for correctness
recommendations.implementation_hint: Test fixture for organization creation, test soft_delete/restore flow, test setting get/set methods, test status transitions

recommendations.recommendation: Create ADR (Architecture Decision Record) documenting multi-tenancy strategy and tenant isolation enforcement
recommendations.rationale: Critical business requirement (data isolation) is documented in code comments but not in formal architecture documentation
recommendations.implementation_hint: Document why tenant filtering is critical, what happens if bypassed, how it's enforced, and potential future improvements

recommendations.recommendation: Add database constraint or check to prevent inconsistent state combinations
recommendations.rationale: status=DELETED can coexist with deleted_at=NULL or vice versa creating ambiguity
recommendations.implementation_hint: Add migration with CheckConstraint ensuring status=DELETED iff deleted_at is NOT NULL, or use model validation

recommendations.recommendation: Consider denormalizing frequently accessed settings or creating separate settings table for high-read scenarios
recommendations.rationale: If organization settings become large or frequently read, JSONField deserialization could impact performance
recommendations.implementation_hint: Monitor in production; if needed, refactor to separate Settings model with ForeignKey and caching

recommendations.recommendation: Add type hints to settings-related methods for IDE support
recommendations.rationale: get_setting and set_setting use generic types; better IDE support with typing.Dict annotations
recommendations.implementation_hint: Use typing.Any or create settings TypedDict for better type safety
